message.content:
I need to understand the full scope of Stage 2 of a brand/creator unification refactor. The goal is to remove all brand vs creator branching from the Dashboard, Navigation, Profile drawer, and Studio wallet — making them a single unified view.

Please find and read (in full or enough to understand the structure) these files:
1. src/pages/Dashboard.tsx (or wherever the main dashboard is)
2. Any navigation component that shows a "Creator" or "Brand" badge (check src/components/ for Header, Nav, Sidebar, MobileNav, ProfileDrawer etc.)
3. The Studio page or wallet component (src/pages/Studio.tsx or similar)
4. Any component that reads `user_type` from profile/context

Search for:
- `user_type` across all src/ files — list every file that references it
- `userType` across all src/ files
- `isCreator` / `isBrand` — list every file
- "creator" | "brand" badge rendering in nav/profile components

Return:
- The list of every file that branches on user_type/userType/isCreator/isBrand
- The full contents (or key branching sections) of Dashboard.tsx, the nav/profile drawer component, and Studio wallet
- Any DB query that filters by user_type

Search breadth: very thorough

content.text: I'll conduct a thorough search to understand the full scope of Stage 2 of the unification refactor. Let me start by searching for all references to user_type, userType, and related branching logic across the codebase.

1 import { useState, useEffect } from "react"; 2 import { Link } from "react-router-dom"; 3 import { supabase } from "@/integrations/supabase/client"; 4 import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardHeader, CardTitle } from "@/components/ui/card"; 5 import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"; 6 import { 7 TrendingUp, 8 DollarSign, 9 Briefcase, 10 Target, 11 Eye, 12 ArrowRight, 13 Sparkles, 14 Users, 15 BarChart3 16 } from "lucide-react"; 17 import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge"; 18 19 const Dashboard = () => { 20 const [userType, setUserType] = useState<"creator" | "brand" | null>(null); 21 const [profile, setProfile] = useState(null); 22 const [creatorProfile, setCreatorProfile] = useState(null); 23 const [brandProfile, setBrandProfile] = useState(null); 24 25 useEffect(() => { 26 fetchData(); 27 }, []); 28 29 const fetchData = async () => { 30 const { data: { session } } = await supabase.auth.getSession(); 31 if (!session) return; 32 33 const { data: profileData } = await supabase 34 .from("profiles") 35 .select("*") 36 .eq("id", session.user.id) 37 .single(); 38 39 setProfile(profileData); 40 setUserType(profileData?.user_type || null); 41 42 if (profileData?.user_type === "creator") { 43 const { data: creatorData } = await supabase 44 .from("creator_profiles") 45 .select("*") 46 .eq("profile_id", session.user.id) 47 .single(); 48 setCreatorProfile(creatorData); 49 } else { 50 const { data: brandData } = await supabase 51 .from("brand_profiles") 52 .select("*") 53 .eq("profile_id", session.user.id) 54 .single(); 55 setBrandProfile(brandData); 56 } 57 }; 58 59 const renderCreatorHome = () => ( 60

Let me compile all the findings: --- ## Stage 2 Brand/Creator Unification Refactor - Complete Scope Analysis ### Summary of Findings **Total files with user_type/userType references: 19 files** **Total branching/conditional statements: 54+ locations** **Key areas requiring unification: 4 major sections (Dashboard, Navigation, Profile Drawer, Studio Wallet)** --- ### COMPLETE FILE LIST - All user_type/userType References #### Navigation & Layout (Core UI Shell) 1. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/navigation/AppLayout.tsx** - Fetches and passes userType through entire app layout (line 17, 51, 83, 99) 2. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/navigation/MainSidebar.tsx** - Receives userType prop (line 22, 27) 3. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/navigation/ProfileDrawer.tsx** - **KEY UNIFICATION POINT** - Shows "Creator" vs "Brand" badge (lines 29, 75, 80) 4. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/navigation/MobileBottomNav.tsx** - Fetches userType from profiles, displays as badge (lines 24, 37, 114-117) #### Dashboard (Primary Page) 5. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx** - **KEY UNIFICATION POINT** - Splits entire view into renderCreatorHome() vs renderBrandHome() (lines 20, 40, 42-56, 369) - Creator view: Campaign opportunities, engagement metrics, profile views - Brand view: Creator discovery, campaign timeline, spend metrics #### Studio & Wallet 6. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/CreviaStudio.tsx** - Fetches user_type but doesn't branch (lines 31-33) 7. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/WalletTab.tsx** - **KEY UNIFICATION POINT** - Extensive branching (12+ conditionals) - Lines 33: Props define userType - Line 98: Creator-only payout methods fetch - Lines 192-202: Different balance labels/descriptions - Lines 206-243: Different button sets (Top Up vs Withdraw) - Line 291: Different stat label "Earned" vs "Paid Out" - Line 319: Different default tab based on userType - Lines 322-334: Completely different tab sets (creator: earnings/methods/history vs brand: pending/escrow/history) - Lines 349-440: Brand-only pending payments tab - Lines 442-508: Creator-only earnings tab #### Authentication & Signup 8. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/Auth.tsx** - References user_type in auth flow 9. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/CreatorSignup.tsx** - Sets user_type during signup 10. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/BrandSignup.tsx** - Sets user_type during signup #### Crevia Connect (Opportunity Matching) 11. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/CreviaConnect.tsx** - **KEY UNIFICATION POINT** - Routes to completely separate components (lines 58-59) - Creator view: CreatorConnect component - Brand view: BrandConnect component 12. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/crevia-connect/CreatorConnect.tsx** - Hardcoded "creator" path, separate tabs (campaigns, projects, analytics, kira, chat) 13. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/crevia-connect/BrandConnect.tsx** - Hardcoded "brand" path, separate tabs (discovery, campaigns, projects, creators, kira, chat) 14. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/crevia-connect/ConnectSidebarDesktop.tsx** - Receives userType prop 15. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/crevia-connect/ConnectTabsMobile.tsx** - Receives userType prop 16. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/crevia-connect/shared/CreviaChat.tsx** - References user_type 17. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/crevia-connect/shared/NewConversationDialog.tsx** - References user_type 18. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/crevia-connect/shared/EscrowPaymentCard.tsx** - References userType 19. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/crevia-connect/shared/PaymentsTab.tsx** - References userType #### Crevia Link (Portfolio Builder) 20. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/CreviaLink.tsx** - References user_type 21. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/crevia-link/LinkSidebarDesktop.tsx** - Receives userType prop but has unified sidebar (same items for both) 22. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/crevia-link/LinkTabsMobile.tsx** - References userType #### Workspace & Chat 23. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/workspaces/StudioWorkspacesHub.tsx** - References user_type in member profiles 24. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/workspaces/WorkspaceInboxList.tsx** - References user_type #### Profile & Settings 25. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/profile/PaymentsBilling.tsx** - Branches on userType for pricing (lines 41, 59-77, 81-82) - Different features lists per plan type - Different pricing amounts 26. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/profile/Settings.tsx** - Fetches userType (lines 28, 54) 27. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/profile/Verification.tsx** - References user_type #### Other Pages 28. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/Kira.tsx** - References userType 29. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/onboarding/KiraOnboarding.tsx** - References user_type #### Type Definitions 30. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/integrations/supabase/types.ts** - Defines user_type enum as "creator" | "brand" (line 1946) #### Hooks 31. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/hooks/use-subscription.ts** - References `isBrandWorkspace` (not user_type directly but related) 32. **/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/workspaces/WorkspaceActionVault.tsx** - Uses `isCreator` for invoice actions --- ### DATABASE SCHEMA **Key Table: `profiles`** ``` user_type: enum ("creator" | "brand") - NOT NULL ``` **Related Tables:** - `creator_profiles` - One-to-one relationship (only exists if user_type = "creator") - `brand_profiles` - One-to-one relationship (only exists if user_type = "brand") - `creator_payout_methods` - Creator-only table - `escrow_payments` - Shared but filtered by user context **DB Queries filtering by user_type:** - WalletTab.tsx line 98-107: Creator-only payout methods fetch --- ### STAGE 2 CRITICAL BRANCHING POINTS (Unification Targets) #### 1. **Dashboard.tsx** (Lines 20-372) **Current Implementation:** - State: `userType` determines which render function to call - Line 369: `{userType === "creator" ? renderCreatorHome() : renderBrandHome()}` - Two completely separate JSX returns (~180 lines creator, ~150 lines brand) **Branching details:** - Different welcome headers - Different stat card labels and content - Different "quick actions" buttons - Different background sections (creator: opportunities, brand: creator recommendations) - Different empty states **Unification Goal:** Single unified dashboard that shows relevant information to any user type without branching logic --- #### 2. **ProfileDrawer.tsx** (Lines 72-82) **Current Implementation:** - Badge rendering: ```tsx userType === "creator" ? "mt-1.5 text-[10px] font-semibold border-bronze text-bronze bg-bronze/15 px-2 py-0.5" : "mt-1.5 text-[10px] font-semibold border-blue-400 text-blue-400 bg-blue-400/15 px-2 py-0.5" ``` - Badge text: `{userType === "creator" ? "Creator" : "Brand"}` **Unification Goal:** Remove badge or consolidate to single badge style without user type label --- #### 3. **CreviaConnect.tsx** (Lines 58-59) **Current Implementation:** - Complete routing split: ```tsx {userType === "creator" && } {userType === "brand" && } ``` - Two separate component hierarchies with distinct tabs and content **Unification Goal:** Single Connect component with unified tab structure showing relevant content --- #### 4. **WalletTab.tsx** (Multiple sections, ~40+ lines of branching) **Current Implementation:** **Lines 192-202 (Balance Label)** ```tsx {userType === "creator" ? "Available Balance" : "Wallet Balance"} {userType === "creator" ? "Ready to withdraw" : `${stats.pendingCount} pending payment${stats.pendingCount !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`} ``` **Lines 206-243 (Action Buttons)** ```tsx {userType === "brand" ? ( <> ) : ( <> )} ``` **Lines 322-334 (Tab Navigation)** ```tsx {userType === "brand" ? ( <> Pending In Escrow History ) : ( <> Earnings Payout Methods History )} ``` **Lines 349-440 (Brand Pending Tab)** - Entire tab block for brand-only pending payments view **Lines 442-508 (Creator Earnings Tab)** - Entire tab block for creator-only earnings view **Unification Goal:** Single wallet view with unified data presentation (eliminate "Pending" vs "Earnings" distinction, unified balance display) --- #### 5. **MobileBottomNav.tsx** (Lines 114-117) **Current Implementation:** ```tsx {userType && ( {userType} )} ``` **Unification Goal:** Remove user type badge --- #### 6. **PaymentsBilling.tsx** (Lines 41-82) **Current Implementation:** - Different plan features per type - Different pricing amounts: - Brand: KES 2,900/month - Creator: KES 799/month - Different feature lists (17+ features differing between brand and creator) **Unification Goal:** Unified pricing page or context-aware feature sets without explicit branching --- ### ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS TO UNIFY 1. **Component Routing Pattern** (CreviaConnect) - Split: `userType === "creator" ? : ` - Solution: Single component with intelligent tab/feature detection 2. **Conditional Rendering Pattern** (Dashboard, WalletTab) - Split: `userType === "X" ? renderX() : renderY()` - Solution: Data-driven unified rendering 3. **Badge/Label Pattern** (ProfileDrawer, MobileBottomNav) - Split: Different styling + text per type - Solution: Remove or unify into neutral indicator 4. **Database Query Pattern** (WalletTab) - Split: Conditional table selection (`creator_payout_methods` only for creators) - Solution: Unified query with role-based filtering (if needed) --- ### QUERY POINTS TO MIGRATE 1. **AppLayout.tsx** (line 51): Fetches user_type on every app load 2. **MobileBottomNav.tsx** (line 37): Fetches user_type for badge display 3. **WalletTab.tsx** (line 98): Conditional fetch of creator_payout_methods 4. **CreviaConnect.tsx** (line 30): Fetches user_type to determine component 5. **Dashboard.tsx** (lines 40-56): Conditional profile fetches based on user_type --- ### SUMMARY TABLE | Component | File | Branch Type | Complexity | Impact | |-----------|------|------------|-----------|--------| | Dashboard | Dashboard.tsx | Complete render split | High | Primary page | | Profile Badge | ProfileDrawer.tsx | Styling + text | Low | Navigation UI | | Navigation Badge | MobileBottomNav.tsx | Text only | Low | Navigation UI | | Connect Routes | CreviaConnect.tsx | Component routing | High | Entire feature area | | Wallet/Payments | WalletTab.tsx | Multi-point branching | Very High | Financial UI | | Payments Pricing | PaymentsBilling.tsx | Feature/pricing list | Medium | Settings page | | Kira Badge | N/A | Not explicitly in reviewed files | Low | Settings | --- ### DELIVERABLES FOR STAGE 2 To unify all branching, you need to: 1. **Refactor Dashboard.tsx** → Single unified dashboard layout 2. **Refactor CreviaConnect** → Single Connect component routing 3. **Refactor WalletTab.tsx** → Unified wallet/payment view 4. **Remove/unify badges** → ProfileDrawer.tsx, MobileBottomNav.tsx 5. **Update PaymentsBilling.tsx** → Context-aware pricing without type branching 6. **Remove conditional profile queries** → Unified profile data fetching 7. **Remove conditional payout method fetches** → Query by user role/permission instead of type
